name: Phishing Email Response
description: Response playbook for confirmed phishing emails targeting organisation users. Covers email quarantine, domain blocking, and user notification.

action_sequence.description: Quarantine the phishing email and remove it from all recipient inboxes
Add the sender domain to the email gateway block list
Send a security advisory to the affected user explaining the phishing attempt
Review URL click logs to determine if the user clicked any malicious links
Submit the phishing URL to PhishTank and relevant threat intelligence feeds

name: Compromised Credential Response
description: Response playbook for suspected credential compromise — triggered when anomalous login follows a phishing click on the same identity.

action_sequence.description: Immediately force a password reset on the compromised account
Terminate all active sessions for the affected user across all services
Require step-up / multi-factor authentication for the next login
Review all recent activity on the account for signs of data access or lateral movement
Contact the user directly to confirm whether the access was legitimate

name: Brute Force / Credential Stuffing Response
description: Response playbook for high failed-login rate from a single or rotating set of source IPs, indicating credential stuffing or brute-force attack.

action_sequence.description: Temporarily lock the targeted account(s) for 30 minutes
Enable CAPTCHA challenge for the targeted login endpoint
Apply rate-limiting rules to the source IP range at the firewall
Cross-reference targeted credentials against known breach databases
If attack persists, permanently block the source IP range

name: Anomalous OT/SCADA Access Response
description: CRITICAL: Response playbook for anomalous access to OT/SCADA control endpoints — off-hours access, cross-segment traffic, or command frequency deviation.

action_sequence.description: Immediately isolate the affected OT network segment from the IT network
Page the on-call OT engineer with full alert details and SHAP explanation
Escalate to SOC lead immediately — this is a potential industrial control system compromise
Start packet capture on the affected segment for forensic analysis
Assess whether any control commands were executed and verify physical system state
If confirmed, report the incident to NCIIPC as a Critical Information Infrastructure event

name: Data Exfiltration Response
description: CRITICAL: Response playbook for detected data exfiltration — abnormal outbound volume or entropy from a host suggesting data staging and transfer.

action_sequence.description: Block all outbound traffic from the affected host at the firewall
Isolate the host from the internal network to prevent lateral movement
Take a full disk and memory snapshot of the host for forensic investigation
Open a formal security incident with full timeline and evidence chain
Notify the Data Protection Officer — potential DPDP Act breach notification trigger
Report to CERT-In within the mandatory reporting window

name: DDoS on Public Portal Response
description: Response playbook for DDoS attack detected on a public-facing portal — ingress traffic volume anomaly indicating volumetric or application-layer flood.

action_sequence.description: Enable upstream traffic scrubbing / DDoS mitigation service
Apply aggressive rate-limiting rules on the affected endpoint
If attack originates from specific regions, apply geo-blocking rules
Contact the ISP to request upstream filtering and provide attack signature data
Report the DDoS attack to CERT-In with traffic analysis data
